text.title: event-sourcing-basics Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for event-sourcing-basics as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Mutable event: assigning to event.customer_id after construction.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Breaks immutability guarantee. Any code that cached the event has an inconsistent copy, making replay non-deterministic.

failure-mode.description: Catch-all event: OrderUpdated with entire_order_data: dict containing all order fields.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Loses intent (what specifically changed?), bloats the event log, and makes schema evolution impossible without breaking all existing consumers.

failure-mode.description: Assigning new values to event fields after construction (e.g., event.customer_id = new_id).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Events stored in the event log become inconsistent with in-memory events. Replay produces different state than the original, making the event log unreliable as the source of truth.

failure-mode.description: Using a single event type like OrderUpdated that carries the entire order state as a dict.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Loses intent (which field actually changed?), makes projections load unnecessary data, and makes schema evolution impossible without breaking all consumers that parse the dict.
